questions.question: Explain the switch statement and its advantages over if-else-if ladder.
keyPoints: Switch is a multi-way branching statement that tests expression against constant values
Provides cleaner and more readable code for multiple discrete conditions
Compiler can optimize using jump tables for O(1) performance
Avoids repetitive condition testing present in if-else chains
Easier to maintain when adding or removing cases

questions.question: What is fall-through in switch statements? When is it useful? Provide an example.
keyPoints: Fall-through occurs when break is omitted, execution continues to next case
Useful for grouping multiple case values that require same action
Example: case 1: case 2: case 3: printf("Weekday"); handles Mon, Tue, Wed
Must be intentional and documented with comments
Common in menu-driven programs for related options

questions.question: Write a C program using switch to calculate the area of different shapes (circle, rectangle, triangle).
keyPoints: Use switch to select shape based on user choice
Circle: area = πr², Rectangle: area = l*w, Triangle: area = 0.5*b*h
Include default case for invalid input
Use appropriate data types (float for calculations)
Include necessary header files and proper main function

questions.question: What are the restrictions on case labels in a switch statement?
keyPoints: Case labels must be constant expressions (compile-time constants)
Cannot use variables or function calls as case labels
Must be unique within the same switch block
Only integral types allowed (int, char, enum, short, long)
Floating-point and string types are not permitted

questions.question: Differentiate between switch and if-else statements in C with suitable examples.
keyPoints: Switch works with discrete values, if-else with boolean expressions
Switch expression must be integral type, if-else evaluates any condition
Switch provides jump table optimization, if-else evaluates sequentially
Switch is more readable for many branches, if-else is flexible for complex logic
Example: switch(day) vs if(day>0 && day<7)

questions.question: Explain the role of default case in switch statement. Can it appear anywhere?
keyPoints: Default case executes when no other case matches the expression
It is optional but recommended for robust error handling
Default can appear anywhere in the switch block (beginning, middle, or end)
Conventionally placed at end for readability
If default is not first, include break to prevent unintended fall-through
Output will be: Two, Three, Default (each on new line)
No break statements causes fall-through
Execution starts at matching case (2) and continues through all subsequent cases
This demonstrates the fall-through behavior
To get only 'Two', break must be added after each case

questions.question: Write a complete C program for a simple calculator using switch statement. The program should perform addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division based on user choice.
keyPoints: Include switch to select operation based on operator character
Handle four basic operations with separate cases
Include default case for invalid operator
Handle division by zero case
Use appropriate input/output functions
Structure: read num1, operator, num2, then switch on operator
Print appropriate error messages for invalid input

questions.question: Write a C program using switch to implement a menu-driven program for a simple ATM with options: check balance, deposit, withdraw, and exit.
keyPoints: Use do-while loop for continuous menu display
Switch statement to handle menu options
Maintain balance variable (initialize with some amount)
Check balance: simply display current balance
Deposit: add amount to balance (validate positive)
Withdraw: subtract if sufficient balance, show error otherwise
Exit: terminate the program
Include input validation and appropriate messages

questions.question: Write a C program to convert a given number (1-12) to corresponding month name using switch statement. Handle invalid input with default case.
keyPoints: Accept month number as input
Use switch with 12 cases for each month
Include default case for numbers outside 1-12
Print appropriate month name or error message
Use break after each case to prevent fall-through
Consider including enum for better readability
Handle both valid and invalid scenarios properly

questions.question: Explain with examples: (a) nested switch statements, (b) switch with characters, (c) switch with enum constants.
keyPoints: Nested switch: switch inside another switch, each with independent cases
Character switch: char is promoted to int, so case 'A' works
Enum switch: enum values are integers, can be used directly as cases
Example code for each scenario with explanation
Discuss practical applications and benefits
Show proper use of break in each context
